intro: Python acts as a general-purpose language with applications spanning machine learning, automation, and backend engineering. PHP is purposely engineered and optimized specifically for web development and server-side request rendering. Migration frequently occurs when shifting a system to standard web-hosting environments or integrating into legacy CMS infrastructures optimized for immediate HTTP response cycles.

diffs.to: Dynamic, weakly typed (supports gradual static typing with strict_types).

diffs.from: Persistent application process running an event loop or WSGI server.
diffs.to: Shared-nothing architecture; process boots, executes, and dies per request.

diffs.from: Distinct sequence lists and key-value dictionaries.
diffs.to: Unified associative arrays used for both lists and dictionaries.

examples.title: Associative Data Mapping
examples.description: Creating a dictionary/associative array and looking up values.

examples.title: JSON Decoding
examples.description: Parsing a JSON string into an accessible object or array structure.

tips.title: Enable Strict Types
tips.body: Declare strict_types=1 at the top of PHP files to enforce parameter and return type declarations.

tips.title: Use Unified Arrays
tips.body: Combine Python lists and dicts into PHP's native array structure, keeping track of sequential vs string keys.

tips.title: Mind the Request Lifecycle
tips.body: Remember that state does not persist in memory between HTTP requests in PHP like it does in a Python web server.

tips.title: Prefix Variables with Sigils
tips.body: Prepend all variable allocations and references with a dollar sign ($), unlike Python's bare tokens.
